programmes.show_description: David Attenborough narrates the story of Rodrigo Medellin, Mexico's very own 'Bat Man', who has dedicated his life to saving bats. Now the legendary drink tequila is also at stake.
5/12. The candidates turn petals into profits, selling flowers to the public and corporate clients. But a bunch of problems lead to a pruning in the boardroom.
5/11. As Lord Sugar continues his search for a new business partner, Angela Scanlon and guests Shazia Mirza and Mike Soutar take a look at the candidates' performances.
4/4. Ralph jumps into action when violence escalates, then realises with horror that it is his turn to be hunted. Contains some violence and upsetting scenes.
1/3. A chatbot encourages a teenager to try to kill Queen Elizabeth II. Who is responsible? Contains some strong language and some upsetting scenes.
Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.
Cameron wants his dad to grow up and be more of a father figure to him. Can time in the wild help them reconnect - and put an end to Mark's silly behaviour?
2/5. From the road trip archives, music legends Peter Andre, Martin Kemp and Sinitta swap the studio for the saleroom, vying to be top of the pops. But who will get the number one spot?
31/50. Steve Brown helps a couple wanting space for animals to swap urban Gloucestershire for rural Herefordshire and also visits former escapees who now run a thriving organic farm.
1/6. The Big Cats 24/7 team returns to the Okavango Delta. With bigger cubs to feed, the lions hunt around the clock. New female leopard Lediba has cubs, and cheetah Pobe is back.

8/30. Game show in which players score points by answering questions correctly while avoiding the Impossible answers.
12/45. Jason Manford hosts the quiz where it's not just about finding the right answer, it's finding the best answer that counts.
18/25. Susan Calman hosts the quiz show where being number one is everything. Five contestants compete to take control and knock the Boss out of the game.
18/20. Antiques dealers Adi Higham, James Gooch and Jacqui Harries are in Villeneuve-lés-Avignon to find two hidden treasures each to buy and then sell for a profit online.
Archaeologist, Ben Robinson visits the Yorkshire coastal village of Flamborough and discovers its historic chalk buildings.
8/8. Documentary. The team dives into one of the world's most hostile environments, beneath the polar ice cap, to explore how the ice is shrinking and how this may affect the climate.
Paul Martin and the team are in the seaside town of Bexhill-on-Sea. Their finds include an art deco children's clock and a painting that could be worth thousands.
82/100. Former players Josh Jones, Sarah Keyworth, Melvin Odoom and Kimberly Wyatt test their general knowledge skills on this week's Richard Osman's House of Games: Redemption Week.

programmes.show_description: 83/100. Josh Jones, Sarah Keyworth, Melvin Odoom and Kimberly Wyatt face more questions.
8/20. Vernon Kay quizzes actor Nitin Ganatra, the Rev Kate Bottley and Alex Gray, aka Gladiator Apollo, on their knowledge of Falkirk - with help from unreliable tour guide Paul Gorton.
5/29. The remaining three chefs from Scotland compete over mains and desserts inspired by the British film industry. Who will win, and who must go home?
6/6. Paul heads to the River Ayr for this Grand Tour. Starting on Hareshaw Hill, he uncovers a legacy of mineral wealth and religious conflict before reaching the Clyde coast.
1/3. Two reporters chase a scoop in Syria but fall into the sights of jihadists. Contains some strong language and disturbing scenes.
2/4. Freya and Jo navigate grief, missteps and misunderstandings. Abe's staged proposal sparks confusion and unexpected tensions. Contains strong language.
Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Katie Razzall.   Followed by Weather.
John Simpson analyses whether the Iranian regime will survive, and looks at what Israel and the United States are aiming to achieve in their war with Iran.
1/3. A look into the beginnings of the Gloriavale community and how it evolved from its socialist Christian beginnings to the extreme place it now occupies.
2/3. A dogged journalist is determined to uncover the shocking truth about Gloriavale. Contains scenes which some viewers may find upsetting.
3/3. The community reels at the loss of its charismatic leader Hopeful Christian, while two former children of Gloriavale expose one of its most shocking secrets.
2/6. Plans for Rachel's recovery are set in motion, and the family face an uncertain future. Contains strong language and some upsetting scenes.
4/29. Four chefs compete for Scotland with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by the British film industry. Who will be eliminated?
Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.
Julie and Jason were happily married for 27 years. But Julie's experience of going through menopause nearly tore them apart. Can time in the wild help get their spark back?
3/5. Past road trip competitors and comedy icons Jennifer Saunders, Darren Harriott and Phill Jupitus trade punchlines for profits, hunting for antique treasures. No hecklers, please!
32/50. Nicki Chapman is in the West Country, helping three generations from the West Midlands make a Devon escape. She also tries her hand at weaving with a local textile designer.
3/4. Monty looks back at some of the remarkable gardeners shining a light on how people can grow their own nutritious food fresh from the garden.

programmes.show_name: Villages by the Sea - Cley next the sea
programmes.show_description: Archaeologist Ben Robinson discovers how Dutch settlers in the 1500s transformed the Norfolk village of Cley next the Sea.
1/3. Documentary series. Using the latest 3D technology, Alexander Armstrong and Dr Michael Scott uncover the hidden history in Naples from its earliest Greek and Roman origins.
